stories.name: docker-compose-relay-setup
stories.title: Add docker-compose.e2e.yml with ephemeral strfry relay
stories.description: Create docker-compose.e2e.yml defining a strfry relay on port 7777 with a tmpfs (ephemeral) volume so relay state is wiped on every run. Reuse ./strfry.conf. Add Makefile targets e2e-up and e2e-down following the existing phony + ## help comment pattern and using docker compose V2 syntax (space, not hyphen).

includes: docker-compose.e2e.yml with relay service (strfry image, port 7777, tmpfs volume, strfry.conf mount)
Makefile target e2e-up: docker compose -f docker-compose.e2e.yml up -d
Makefile target e2e-down: docker compose -f docker-compose.e2e.yml down -v
Both targets annotated with ## help comments and listed under .PHONY
excludes: Full make e2e orchestration target (that is Story 03)

scenarios.name: relay-starts-and-accepts-websocket
scenarios.description: Verify docker-compose.e2e.yml brings up the relay and a WebSocket client can connect, then down -v leaves no persistent volume.
user_steps: Run: npx wscat -c ws://localhost:7777 --execute '' (or equivalent one-shot connection check)
assertions: wscat connection exits without 'connection refused' error
docker volume ls output does not contain a relay data volume after down -v

stories.notes: Foundation story. No application code changes. strfry.conf already exists and accepts all events. docker compose V2 (space) as established by exploration patterns.

stories.name: bunker-fixture
stories.title: Write e2e/fixtures/bunker.mjs NIP-46 bunker fixture
stories.description: Create e2e/fixtures/bunker.mjs as a Node ESM module (top-level await). It instantiates NDKNip46Backend from @nostr-dev-kit/ndk (already at ^3.0.3 in package.json), connects to ws://localhost:7777, and starts the bunker. The file declares E2E_BUNKER_NSEC as a hardcoded constant (deterministic test private key), derives the corresponding public key, exports E2E_BUNKER_URL as `bunker://<HEX_PUBKEY>?relay=ws%3A%2F%2Flocalhost%3A7777`, and logs a ready message to stdout when the backend is active.

includes: e2e/fixtures/bunker.mjs with E2E_BUNKER_NSEC constant (hardcoded test nsec)
NDKNip46Backend instantiation connecting to ws://localhost:7777
E2E_BUNKER_URL export derived from E2E_BUNKER_NSEC public key
stdout ready log when bunker is listening
Script is runnable standalone: node e2e/fixtures/bunker.mjs
excludes: NDK headless client fixture (that is Story 03)
Any Makefile targets beyond what Story 01 adds

scenarios.name: bunker-logs-ready
scenarios.description: With relay running, execute bunker.mjs standalone and confirm it logs a ready message within 5 seconds and E2E_BUNKER_URL matches expected pattern.
user_steps: Wait up to 5 seconds for 'ready' in stdout
assertions: stdout contains a ready/listening message within 5 seconds

stories.notes: NDK ^3.0.3 is already installed (exploration: package.json). NDKNip46Backend is part of that package. File uses .mjs extension because it needs top-level await and project is 'type': 'module'. The deterministic nsec ensures E2E_BUNKER_URL is stable across runs.

stories.name: playwright-setup
stories.title: Install Playwright and wire globalSetup, globalTeardown, and NDK fixture
stories.description: Add @playwright/test and serve to devDependencies. Write playwright.config.ts at the repo root with baseURL: 'http://localhost:3100', testDir: './e2e/tests', globalSetup, and globalTeardown. Write the globalSetup module that: (1) builds the app with NODE_ENV=test NEXT_PUBLIC_RELAYS=ws://localhost:7777 npx next build, (2) starts node e2e/fixtures/bunker.mjs as a child process and waits for its ready log, (3) starts npx serve out -l 3100 and waits for HTTP 200 on http://localhost:3100. Write globalTeardown to kill the child processes. Write e2e/fixtures/ndk-client.ts as a Playwright fixture extending test with an ndkClient fixture (headless NDK with NDKPrivateKeySigner, second deterministic test keypair). Add make e2e-install target (npm install + npx playwright install --with-deps chromium) and make e2e target that orchestrates: e2e-up, globalSetup (via playwright), npx playwright test, globalTeardown (via playwright), e2e-down. Verify make e2e exits 0 with an empty test suite.

includes: package.json devDependencies: @playwright/test, serve
playwright.config.ts: baseURL http://localhost:3100, testDir ./e2e/tests, globalSetup, globalTeardown
e2e/global-setup.ts: app build (NODE_ENV=test NEXT_PUBLIC_RELAYS=ws://localhost:7777), bunker child process, serve child process, readiness waits
e2e/global-teardown.ts: kill bunker and serve processes
e2e/fixtures/ndk-client.ts: Playwright test fixture with headless NDK, NDKPrivateKeySigner (second test keypair), ndk.connect()
Makefile target e2e-install: npm install + npx playwright install --with-deps chromium (with ## comment, .PHONY)
Makefile target e2e: e2e-up + npx playwright test + e2e-down in sequence (with ## comment, .PHONY)
excludes: Actual test spec files (those are Stories 04 and 05)

test_fixtures.description: Headless NDK client (e2e/fixtures/ndk-client.ts) using a second deterministic test keypair. Acts as a second Nostr user for relay event verification in tests. Not a replacement for any production component — permanent test infrastructure.
test_fixtures.reason: Cannot use a real user's keypair in CI; fixture provides a stable, isolated second identity for relay-level assertions.

scenarios.name: empty-suite-exits-zero
scenarios.description: With no test spec files present, make e2e builds the app, starts services, runs Playwright (zero tests), and exits 0.
user_steps: Ensure e2e/tests/ directory is empty (no .spec.ts files)
assertions: Output shows relay started (docker compose up)
Output shows app built (next build completed)
Output shows 0 tests passed (Playwright empty run)
Output shows relay stopped (docker compose down)

stories.notes: This is the most complex story as it wires all infrastructure together. The Makefile e2e target must handle failure gracefully — if playwright test fails, e2e-down should still run (use make -k or a shell trap pattern). Readiness checks in globalSetup: poll http://localhost:3100 with fetch() until 200, poll relay with a WebSocket connection until open.

stories.name: auth-e2e-tests
stories.title: Write auth.spec.ts covering NIP-46 bunker authentication flows
stories.description: Write e2e/tests/auth.spec.ts with three test cases: (1) full auth flow — navigate to app, select bunker tab, paste E2E_BUNKER_URL, click Connect, assert pubkey chip visible in header and localStorage has notestr-nip46-payload; (2) session restore — after auth, call page.reload() and assert pubkey chip still visible without re-authenticating; (3) disconnect — after auth, click disconnect, assert pubkey chip gone, sign-in UI visible, and localStorage key cleared. Each test clears localStorage before starting via page.evaluate(() => localStorage.clear()).

includes: e2e/tests/auth.spec.ts with three test cases: full auth flow, session restore, disconnect
Use of E2E_BUNKER_URL (imported from bunker.mjs or set via playwright.config.ts env)
localStorage assertions via page.evaluate()
pubkey chip selector assertion (page.locator for the chip element)
sign-in screen visibility assertion after disconnect
localStorage.clear() before each test
excludes: NIP-07 browser extension auth (out of scope for this epic)
nostrconnect:// URL auth (out of scope for this epic)

scenarios.name: bunker-auth-flow
scenarios.description: User navigates to app, selects bunker tab, pastes URL, connects, sees pubkey chip.
user_steps: Wait for pubkey chip to appear in header

scenarios.name: session-restore-after-reload
scenarios.description: After authenticating, page.reload() still shows pubkey chip via restoreNip46Session().

scenarios.name: disconnect-clears-session
scenarios.description: Clicking disconnect removes pubkey chip and clears localStorage.

stories.notes: Requires Story 03 (playwright.config.ts and globalSetup) to be complete before this story can be verified. The auth helper (a shared function calling the bunker flow) should be extracted to e2e/fixtures/ if used in Story 05 as well.

stories.name: groups-and-tasks-e2e-tests
stories.title: Write groups.spec.ts and tasks.spec.ts for group/task creation flows
stories.description: Write e2e/tests/groups.spec.ts: use an auth helper to pre-authenticate via bunker before each test, then create a group with a known name and assert the name appears in the sidebar list. Write e2e/tests/tasks.spec.ts: pre-authenticate and select a group, create a task with a known title, and assert the task card appears in the 'To Do' column. Both spec files clear localStorage between tests.

includes: e2e/tests/groups.spec.ts: beforeEach auth via bunker helper, create group named 'E2E Test Group', assert sidebar shows 'E2E Test Group'
e2e/tests/tasks.spec.ts: beforeEach auth + group selection, create task titled 'E2E Task', assert card in 'To Do' column
Shared auth helper (e2e/fixtures/auth-helper.ts or inline) that performs bunker auth flow
localStorage.clear() and IndexedDB clear between tests to avoid MLS state bleed
excludes: Drag-and-drop to 'In Progress' column (optional enhancement, out of scope for initial infrastructure)
Group invitation with second user (ndkClient fixture available but not required for initial stories)

scenarios.name: group-creation-appears-in-sidebar
scenarios.description: Authenticated user creates a group and sees it in the sidebar.
assertions: page.locator('text=E2E Test Group') is visible within the sidebar element

scenarios.name: task-creation-appears-in-todo-column
scenarios.description: Authenticated user creates a task in a group and sees it in 'To Do'.
user_steps: Select group 'E2E Test Group' from sidebar

stories.notes: Requires Story 03 and Story 04 to be complete. The auth helper from Story 04 should be reused here. IndexedDB holds MLS group state (marmot-ts uses idb-keyval) so tests should call page.evaluate(() => indexedDB.deleteDatabase('...')) in teardown to prevent cross-test MLS state bleed. The exact IndexedDB database name must be confirmed by inspecting the marmot-ts/idb-keyval usage in src/marmot/client.tsx.

destination.artifact: auth.spec.ts pastes E2E_BUNKER_URL into the bunker input

intermediaries.artifact: e2e/global-setup.ts spawns bunker.mjs as child process, waits for ready log

destination.artifact: auth.spec.ts relies on running bunker for NIP-46 handshake

intermediaries.artifact: Makefile e2e target runs make e2e-up before npx playwright test

source.artifact: auth.spec.ts bunker auth flow (shared auth helper)

destination.artifact: groups.spec.ts and tasks.spec.ts beforeEach auth helper

dependency_notes: Stories 01 and 02 are independent of each other and can be implemented in parallel. Story 03 depends on 01 and 02 being present (it wires them together via globalSetup and make targets), but can be developed with stubs if needed. Stories 04 and 05 require Story 03 to be complete for verification, but their spec files can be written independently. Story 05 reuses the auth helper introduced in Story 04.
mock_resolution_plan: No temporary mocks are needed. All cross-story dependencies use permanent test fixtures (NDK headless client) or direct imports (E2E_BUNKER_URL constant). The relay and bunker are real implementations, not mocks.
